target: WIN-038 detail_evidence — replace the EXP-198 preemptive transverse-mode paragraph (paragraph 4 of detail_evidence) with an enhanced version that (a) anchors argument (i) to WIN-029’s 5.3% agreement between 9,572 km back-derivation and 9,086 km model parameterization (ISS-1310), and (b) adds a new sub-argument (iv) closing the cylindrical-waveguide Bessel-mode disc-cavity escape (ISS-1309). All other paragraphs of detail_evidence remain unchanged.
patch_scope: Only the fourth paragraph of WIN-038.detail_evidence (the EXP-198 insertion, beginning with “The ‘quarter-wave doesn’t apply at planetary scale’ escape is closed in advance” and ending with “either the pole-floor holds (quarter-wave) or the globe is assumed (transverse).”). The three preceding paragraphs (pole-floor impossibility, averaging backup, dome’s own triple-count) and the fifth paragraph (stability is ionospheric physics) are unchanged.
anchor_old_start: The “quarter-wave doesn’t apply at planetary scale” escape is closed in advance (preemptive).
anchor_old_end: either the pole-floor holds (quarter-wave) or the globe is assumed (transverse).
replacement_html: The "quarter-wave doesn’t apply at planetary scale" escape is closed in advance (preemptive). The strongest available defense against the pole-floor argument is to say f = c/(4H) is a parallel-plate simplification that breaks down at planetary scale, and the real Schumann mechanism is a transverse (TM) mode running around the cavity’s horizontal dimension: fn = (c / 2πR) × √(n(n+1)) ≈ 10.59 Hz for n = 1 on a 6,371 km sphere (Schumann 1952; formalized in Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002), with the observed 7.83 Hz produced by ionospheric damping. This is correct globe physics — and it cannot rescue the dome for four compounding reasons. (i) The dome is already on record using quarter-wave, and WIN-029’s numerical match vanishes if the dome retreats. The model page (V50.6, “Schumann Cavity Height”) writes H = c / (4 × f_Schumann) = 299,792 / (4 × 7.83) = 9,572 km, and WIN-029 is the credit claim for that exact inversion. A retreat to transverse modes does not save WIN-038 in isolation; it requires the author to simultaneously withdraw WIN-029 and delete the 9,572 km firmament-height figure propagated throughout the model. And the retreat is not free: WIN-029’s “confirmed” status rests specifically on the 5.3% agreement between the 9,572 km back-derivation and the model’s 9,086 km parameterization. That quantitative match is the entire reason WIN-029 is scored as a confirmation rather than a tautological unit conversion — and a retreat from quarter-wave erases the 5.3% agreement entirely, because the transverse-mode formula has no cavity-height output at all. WIN-029 stripped of the 9,572 km number collapses from “confirmation of a 9,086 km firmament” to the qualitative restatement “a conductive cavity exists,” which every globe textbook already grants. You cannot have quarter-wave for the back-derivation you want and transverse-mode for the forward-derivation you need. (ii) Transverse mode imports a globe fingerprint. The spherical transverse formula reproduces 7.83 Hz only when the wavelength-setting length equals Earth’s circumference, 2πREarth = 40,030 km using the mean-radius R = 6,371 km (the WGS84 equatorial circumference is 40,075 km and is the figure quoted in STEELMAN-03; the AE-projection identity disc_radius = π × R holds under either choice). The dome’s published disc diameter is 2 × 20,015 = 40,030 km — identical to four significant figures, because disc_radius = π × REarth is exactly the azimuthal-equidistant projection identity. Plugging dome disc diameter into the globe’s transverse formula is the classic tautology flagged in Part 6 (STEELMAN-03: Tesla Disc Diameter Lock): a number built from globe geometry by construction cannot independently confirm dome geometry. (iii) The dome’s non-uniform H(r) violates the transverse-mode boundary conditions. The fn ≈ 10.59 Hz formula assumes a closed, approximately uniform spherical shell; the dome’s H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r / 8,619) km varies by roughly a factor of ten across the disc (8,537 km at the pole down to ≈836 km at the 20,015 km rim) and terminates at an open rim. Borrowing the sphere’s mode formula without the sphere’s geometry is precisely the silent formula-switch already documented in Section 2.1. (iv) The natural disc-cavity escape — cylindrical-waveguide Bessel modes — also fails. A sophisticated defender may object that the honest transverse analog for a disc-with-ceiling is neither vertical quarter-wave nor spherical TM but a cylindrical-waveguide mode governed by Bessel-function zeros: f = jn,m · c / (2πa). For the dome’s disc of radius a = 20,015 km, the leading cutoffs are TM01 at j0,1 · c / (2πa) = 2.4048 · 299,792 / (2π · 20,015) ≈ 5.73 Hz, TM11 at ≈ 9.13 Hz, and TE11 at ≈ 4.39 Hz (Bessel zeros: DLMF §10.21; cylindrical-waveguide formula: Collin, Field Theory of Guided Waves, 2nd ed., Ch. 5). None match 7.83 Hz from dome geometry alone, and the one closest to it (TM11 at 9.13 Hz) requires the same a = 20,015 km that is π · REarth by the AE-projection identity — the globe fingerprint is imported a second time. There is no mode formula the dome has published, nor any it could borrow from globe physics or cylindrical-waveguide theory, that reaches 7.83 Hz from dome geometry alone: either the pole-floor holds (quarter-wave), or the globe is assumed (spherical transverse), or the disc-cavity Bessel modes miss by 20–45% while still requiring globe-circumference geometry to even be written down.
holes_addressed: ISS-1309 (curmudgeon c5 holes_found[0], moderate): “no configuration” closing overclaim leaves Bessel-mode disc-cavity route unaddressed — closed by new sub-argument (iv) with explicit TM01 (5.73 Hz), TM11 (9.13 Hz), TE11 (4.39 Hz) calculations and globe-fingerprint closure via the a=20,015 km = π·R_Earth identity.

ISS-1310 (curmudgeon c5 holes_found[1], moderate): argument (i) “must withdraw WIN-029” structurally strong but under-quantified — now anchored to the 5.3% agreement between 9,572 km back-derivation and 9,086 km model parameterization, with explicit statement that the transverse-mode formula has no cavity-height output so the quantitative match evaporates entirely.

curmudgeon c5 holes_found[2] (minor): 40,030 vs 40,075 km “Earth’s circumference” asymmetry with STEELMAN-03 — harmonized via parenthetical acknowledging both (mean-radius 40,030 km; WGS84 equatorial 40,075 km) and noting the AE-projection identity disc_radius = π·R holds regardless of which R is chosen.
curmudgeon c5 holes_found[3] (minor): spherical TM formula citation — added Schumann 1952 attribution and kept Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 link as formalization anchor.
not_addressed: curmudgeon c5 holes_found[5] (minor): TLDR coverage of preemptive argument — deliberately deferred. The TLDR already says “sits below that floor at every single point” (the main claim) and the preemptive paragraph is detail-level content. Adding a TLDR clause risks pushing the 2–3 sentence budget; the decider can make that call during integration.
curmudgeon c5 holes_found[4] (minor): factor-of-four vs factor-of-ten — already fixed in the currently-shipped detail_evidence (the field reads ‘factor of ten’), so no action needed in this patch.
new_evidence_added: Explicit cylindrical-waveguide Bessel-zero cutoff frequencies for the dome’s disc radius a=20,015 km: TM01 = 5.73 Hz, TM11 = 9.13 Hz, TE11 = 4.39 Hz (independently verified: j01·c/(2πa) = 2.4048·299,792/(2π·20,015) = 5.732 Hz)
DLMF §10.21 citation for Bessel zero values (authoritative NIST reference)
Collin, Field Theory of Guided Waves, 2nd ed., Ch. 5 citation for cylindrical waveguide TM/TE mode formula
Schumann 1952 attribution for the spherical TM transverse formula
Quantitative anchor: WIN-029’s 5.3% match is the specific reason it is scored as a confirmation; transverse-mode retreat erases the 9,572 km output and therefore erases the 5.3% agreement.

anticipated_objections.objection: The Bessel-mode treatment assumes a perfect conducting-wall cylindrical waveguide. The dome’s H(r) is not flat, so the true mode is a coupled radial-vertical eigenvalue problem that might give 7.83 Hz.
anticipated_objections.response: The coupled eigenvalue problem is monotone in its eigenvalues with respect to the boundary-volume spectrum. The TM01 cutoff 5.73 Hz is a LOWER bound on the physical propagating TM0 mode of a disc with height-modulated upper boundary (the cutoff rises when you break translational invariance along z; it cannot fall below the pure-cylindrical value). The disc’s first allowed TM0 propagating frequency is therefore at or above 5.73 Hz, and TM1/TE1 modes above that. 7.83 Hz sits in the gap between TM01 (5.73) and TM11 (9.13), not on any computed eigenvalue; even a modified eigenvalue problem for the dome’s H(r) would need a fine-tuned coincidence that the dome has never computed or published. The objection is a hypothetical derivation, not a derivation.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 5.3% match between 9,572 and 9,086 km is itself within observational error of ionospheric D-layer height variability — so WIN-029 remains vindicated even if transverse-mode becomes the stated mechanism.
anticipated_objections.response: This is a different claim entirely. D-layer ionospheric height (~70–90 km) is two orders of magnitude smaller than either 9,572 km or 9,086 km; conflating them is a unit-scale error. Moreover, WIN-029’s scorecard entry is specifically ‘H = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km, 5.3% from model’ — the 5.3% refers to the 9,572 km vs 9,086 km match, not to an ionospheric-variability figure. The defender cannot rebrand the denominator after the fact.

anticipated_objections.objection: You have only demonstrated that a few leading modes don’t match 7.83 Hz; higher modes might.
anticipated_objections.response: Bessel zeros are monotonically increasing, so TM_n,m for (n,m) above (1,1) give frequencies strictly above 9.13 Hz. There is no Bessel-zero combination on a 20,015 km disc that lands between 5.73 Hz and 9.13 Hz. The ‘higher modes’ escape is closed by the monotonicity of the zero sequence.

kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon c5 “no configuration” critique is correct: the original EXP-198 binary framing did implicitly privilege two specific mode formulas (vertical quarter-wave and spherical transverse) without closing the Bessel-mode disc-cavity route that a sophisticated defender would actually reach for. The patch acknowledges this rather than papering over it, and closes the gap with specific numerical cutoffs rather than hand-waving. On argument (i), the curmudgeon is likewise correct that the 5.3% anchor — the quantitative reason WIN-029 is a “confirmation” rather than a unit conversion — was the load-bearing number and the integrated paragraph had relegated it to anticipated_objections only. Promoting it into the main text is the right call.

cross_win_consistency.steelman_03_harmonization: The parenthetical ‘mean-radius R = 6,371 km; WGS84 equatorial circumference is 40,075 km and is the figure quoted in STEELMAN-03’ acknowledges the 40,030 vs 40,075 asymmetry without requiring a STEELMAN-03 edit. If the decider prefers, STEELMAN-03 can be edited to use 40,030 km with a parenthetical for 40,075 km instead — either direction closes the gap.
cross_win_consistency.affects_other_wins: None. The patch is confined to WIN-038.detail_evidence paragraph 4. WIN-029 and WIN-002 are referenced but not modified. The 5.3% figure is already present in WIN-029’s own data and the dome’s published scorecard.

defense_neutralization.notes: Curmudgeon c5 rated defense_survives=2 against the current (EXP-198-integrated) paragraph; the surviving defense was precisely the Bessel-mode gap and the under-anchored (i). With those closed, analyst estimate is defense_survives<=1 (residual risk is only the ‘coupled eigenvalue problem might accidentally give 7.83 Hz’ speculative objection, answered in anticipated_objections[0] and structurally defeated by Bessel-zero monotonicity).
